name: Sant Pere de Serraïma and Sèquia de Manresa Loop
name_original: Sant Pere Sarraïma- circular gravel por la acequia de Manresa
description: Cycle the Sant Pere Sarraïma circular gravel route, a moderate 20.3-mile (32.7 km) loop via the historic Manresa acequia.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Sallent offers diverse terrain within the high-mountain landscapes of the Aragonese Pyrenees. Situated at an altitude of 1,305 meters in the Tena Valley, the region features dramatic Pyrenean scenery with peaks exceeding 3,000 meters. The area is characterized by glacial lakes, prominent reservoirs like Lanuza, and rivers such as the Gállego and Aguas Limpias, which flow through lush forests and expansive pastures. Challenging mountain passes, including the Col du Portalet, are central to many routes. Best touring cycling routes around Sallent The most popular touring cycling route is Sant Pere de Serraïma and Sèquia de Manresa Loop, a 20.3 miles (32.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 32 minutes to complete. This moderate route features varied terrain and scenic views. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Corbatera Pond – Corbatera Canal loop from Sallent, a moderate 17.7 miles (28.5 km) path. This route offers picturesque views of the Corbatera Pond and canal. Local touring cyclists also love the Santa Susanna de Cornet – Sallent loop from Sallent, a 19.4 miles (31.2 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 45 minutes. Touring cycling around Sallent is defined by high-mountain scenery, glacial lakes, and challenging mountain passes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult ascents. The routes in Sallent are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 50 reviews. More than 3000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Sallent's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Sallent?
A: Sallent offers a diverse network of over 20 touring cycling routes. These routes cater to various skill levels, ranging from moderate loops to more challenging ascents through the high-mountain landscapes of the Aragonese Pyrenees.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Sallent?
A: The terrain around Sallent is highly varied, characterized by dramatic high-mountain scenery, including peaks exceeding 3,000 meters. You'll encounter routes with significant ascents, scenic views of glacial lakes and reservoirs like Lanuza, and paths winding through lush forests and expansive pastures along rivers such as the Gállego and Aguas Limpias.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Sallent?
A: Yes, Sallent features several moderate routes ideal for those looking for a less strenuous ride. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974981666/sant-pere-de-serraima-and-sequia-de-manresa-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Pere de Serraïma and Sèquia de Manresa Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 20.3-mile (32.7 km) trail with varied terrain. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33702237" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corbatera Pond – Corbatera Canal loop from Sallent&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 17.7-mile (28.5 km) path offering picturesque views.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders in Sallent?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, Sallent offers several difficult routes. The region is known for its demanding mountain passes, such as the Col du Portalet. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38226536" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Susanna de Cornet – Sallent loop from Sallent&lt;/a&gt;, a 19.4-mile (31.2 km) trail leading through varied and often demanding landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Sallent?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Sallent are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974981666/sant-pere-de-serraima-and-sequia-de-manresa-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Pere de Serraïma and Sèquia de Manresa Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24575406" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corbatera Canal – Bòbila Wetlands loop from Sallent&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 42.5 km route.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on the touring cycling routes in Sallent?
A: Sallent's touring cycling routes are renowned for their stunning panoramic views. You'll be treated to breathtaking high-mountain vistas, picturesque glacial lakes, and prominent reservoirs like Lanuza. Many routes also offer scenic perspectives of the Tena Valley, lush forests, and the winding Gállego and Aguas Limpias rivers.

Q: What attractions or landmarks can I see near the cycling routes in Sallent?
A: While cycling around Sallent, you can explore various historical and natural attractions. Notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4925300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basilica of Santa Maria de la Seu de Manresa&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3826689" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pont Vell de Manresa&lt;/a&gt;, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3020074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talamanca Castle&lt;/a&gt;. For natural beauty, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4018958" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collbaix Summit&lt;/a&gt; or the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5145509" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque de l'Agulla&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Sallent?
A: Given Sallent's high-mountain location in the Aragonese Pyrenees, the best time for touring cycling is typically from late spring through early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is generally more stable, and mountain passes are clear of snow, offering optimal conditions for enjoying the diverse terrain and stunning scenery.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Sallent?
A: The touring cycling routes in Sallent are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the dramatic Pyrenean scenery, the variety of terrain, and the challenging mountain passes that make for a rewarding experience.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling options in Sallent?
A: While many routes in Sallent involve significant elevation changes, some moderate loops can be suitable for families with older children or those accustomed to cycling. Look for routes described as 'moderate' with less extreme elevation gain, focusing on scenic reservoir views or flatter sections of the valleys.

Q: Where can I find parking near the touring cycling routes in Sallent?
A: Sallent de Gállego, as a hub for outdoor activities in the Tena Valley, typically offers parking facilities within the town or at common starting points for popular routes. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for designated parking areas, especially near trailheads or points of interest.

Q: How long do touring cycling routes in Sallent typically take to complete?
A: The duration of touring cycling routes in Sallent varies significantly based on distance, elevation, and your cycling pace. Moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974981666/sant-pere-de-serraima-and-sequia-de-manresa-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Pere de Serraïma and Sèquia de Manresa Loop&lt;/a&gt; (32.7 km) can take around 2 hours 30 minutes, while longer or more challenging routes will naturally require more time.
tips: Cogulló Mountain. It is a mountain of saline waste caused by the Sallent Mine. It is planned to eliminate the waste in the next 50 years, that is if they manage to close the mine.

Mountain of saline and other waste created between 1945 and 1977.
The La Botjosa salt dump measures 775 meters long by an average width of 150 meters, occupies 13.5 Ha and its content is estimated at around 4 million tons of salty waste.

Since the start of the new Cogulló dump in 1977, the potash mining of Sallent and Balsareny stopped dumping saline waste in La Botjosa. Since then, a considerable portion of the saline residues dumped in the La Botjosa dump has disappeared by dissolution in rainwater and air humidity, as evidenced by the geomorphologies in lapiaz on the slopes and dame coiffée where an object acting as an umbrella has protected the salt below
Hermitage dating from the year 1021 located in Sallent in the Bages region listed as Architectural Heritage of Catalonia.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Sant Pere Sarraïma gravel route?
faqs.answer: This route is officially rated as moderate for touring cycling. While some gravel routes in Spain can be challenging, this one is accessible to cyclists with some experience.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route follows the Manresa acequia, an irrigation channel, so you'll find relatively flat or gently sloping sections along the watercourse. As a gravel route, expect varied terrain including wide trails, and some climbs and descents, offering diverse landscapes.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Sant Pere Sarraïma route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several interesting spots. Keep an eye out for the Views of Montaña del Cogulló from the Manresa canal, the historical La Botjosa salt dump, and the serene Sant Pere de Serraïma Hermitage.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Sant Pere Sarraïma- circular gravel via the Manresa acequia is a circular route, meaning it loops back to its starting point.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details aren't provided, as a circular route, you can typically find suitable parking near the start/end point in the vicinity of Sant Pere Sarraïma or along the Manresa acequia. Look for public parking areas in nearby towns or designated trail access points.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit to cycle the Sant Pere Sarraïma- circular gravel via the Manresa acequia route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Gravel cycling in this region is generally enjoyable during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be hot, and winter might bring colder, potentially wet conditions, though the route remains accessible year-round.

faqs.question: Is the Sant Pere Sarraïma gravel route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are often allowed on public trails in Spain, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially when passing through populated areas or near agricultural land. Be mindful of other cyclists and local wildlife.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with the Camí Ramader de Marina, the Camí Natural de la Sèquia, and the Camí del Llobregat, among others.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners in touring cycling?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty rating and varied terrain, this route is best suited for cyclists with some experience. Beginners might find certain sections challenging due to elevation changes and gravel surfaces, but it's not considered an advanced route.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a day of cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: For a comfortable ride, we recommend bringing plenty of water, snacks, a basic repair kit for your bike, appropriate cycling attire, sun protection, and a fully charged phone for navigation and emergencies. A helmet is always advised.

waypoints.name: Views of Montaña del Cogulló from the Manresa canal
